title: Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms - Rachel Taylor MP
h1: Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms
meta_description: Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth
text_excerpt: Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms - Rachel Taylor MP Skip to main content Rachel Taylor MP Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth Join Get Involved Donate About HS2 Get involved Monthly newsletter Contact News My views Surgeries and events More Menu 1st May, 2026 Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms Rachel has held two public meetings at Kingsbury School and Nicholas Chamberlaine School to discuss the government’s proposed changes to the SEND system with parents and teaching staff. The government is investing billions to support children with special educational needs and/or disabilities including £200 million to train teachers, £3.7 billion to create over 60,000 more specialist places and £1.8 billion to create our new ‘Experts at Hand’ service to bring education and health professionals into schools. Speaking after the events, Rachel said: “I want to thank everyone who took part in these events, they were really useful to understand your views. Delivering change in the SEND system to ensure it delivers for families and SEND children is one of my biggest priorities as an MP, so I’m pleased at the positive response these reforms have had and will be sharing all your feedback with the government.” If you were not able to attend and would like to share your views then you still have time to do so. You can submit your thoughts and any concerns to the consultation by clicking here. Rachel Taylor MP Contact details Email: rachel.taylor.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Martin Short at 69 Ratcliffe Rd, Atherstone, CV9 1LF ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
